title: Global all-slice replay-tangent projection
body: Let r be the arithmetic mean gradient of the six frozen replay validation-window losses at the committed pre-attempt state, and let d(alpha) be the weight displacement produced by the Q2.5 AdamW trial at scale alpha. Q2.6 uses d'(alpha) = d(alpha) - max(0, dot(r,d(alpha))/dot(r,r)) r, retaining the scaled trial's moments. It evaluates alpha in {1, 1/2, ..., 1/128} and commits the first projected finite candidate whose directly measured immutable-baseline replay increase is at most 0.015.
claims: When projection applies, the projected displacement is first-order tangent to the mean replay loss; when the dot product is non-positive, the displacement is unchanged.
Weights and both AdamW moment arrays restore together before every retry or outer rejection.
The first-order tangent condition never substitutes for the unchanged direct cumulative functional guard.
